title: quercus rotundifolia f. calicina
intro: Quercus rotundifolia f. calicina, also known as Cork Oak, is a rare and lesser-known variant of the Quercus rotundifolia species, characterized by its unique, rounded leaves and distinctive calycinous growth habit. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of tannins and other bioactive compounds. Consuming Quercus rotundifolia f. calicina has been associated with various health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, enhanced immune function, and reduced risk of certain types of cancer. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to support overall well-being and address specific health concerns, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
